prediction.summary: A critical account takeover incident involving user 'sarah.chen' has been detected. The attack began with an MFA bypass request followed by an impossible travel alert, indicating simultaneous logins from London and Singapore. The attacker, operating from Singapore (198.18.50.22), proceeded to create email forwarding rules to an external address and rules to delete security alerts, before connecting to the VPN and SSH bastion. Automated systems have flagged this as a T1078 Valid Accounts compromise and initiated a password reset.

timeline.event: Successful authentication for user sarah.chen from London (203.0.113.88).
MFA bypass requested for user sarah.chen from London (203.0.113.88) due to push_fatigue.
Successful authentication for user sarah.chen from Singapore (198.18.50.22) on a new device.
IMPOSSIBLE_TRAVEL alert triggered for sarah.chen (London to Singapore in 2 minutes).
User sarah.chen created an inbox rule to forward all emails to attacker@dropmail.cc.
User sarah.chen created an inbox rule to delete security alerts.
User sarah.chen connected to VPN from 198.18.50.22 (first_seen=true).
SSH publickey authentication accepted for sarah.chen from 198.18.50.22 on bastion host.
SIEM identified account takeover indicators: impossible travel, MFA anomaly, and mail forwarding.
CRITICAL SIEM alert for user sarah.chen, technique T1078 Valid Accounts compromise.
Automated response forced an admin password reset for sarah.chen.
IDS reported a session hijack risk score of 92 for user sarah.chen from source 198.18.50.22.

recommendations: Verify the legitimacy of the initial login from London (203.0.113.88) and investigate the MFA bypass request for 'sarah.chen'.
Immediately revoke all active sessions for 'sarah.chen' and ensure the password reset was successful and effective across all integrated systems.
Investigate all activities performed by 'sarah.chen' from IP 198.18.50.22 on the VPN and SSH bastion, including command execution, file access, and any lateral movement.
Block the malicious source IP (198.18.50.22) at network perimeter controls (firewall, security groups).
Analyze the created email forwarding rule to 'attacker@dropmail.cc' and the rule to delete security alerts for further indicators of compromise and to prevent data exfiltration.
